content:
# Gas Chromatography

**Summary**: A technique for separating, identifying, and quantifying components in a mixture.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[2015-danmaliki|Danmaliki, 2015]] used GC-SCD to monitor the level of sulfur during adsorption experiments.

- [[2016-sidaoui|Sidaoui, 2016]] used gas-chromatography to analyze the chemical composition of waste oil.

- [[2020-mohammed|Mohammed, 2020]] used gas_chromatography to confirm the generation of nitrogen gas during thermochemical reactions.

- [[2020-usman|Usman, 2020]] applied gas chromatography to determine CPI and Pr/Ph ratios in bitumen extracts.

- [[2023-al-eid|Al-Eid, 2023]] applied gas-chromatography to analyze hydrocarbon composition and detect evaporative loss.

- [[2024-moh-ali|Moh-Ali, 2024]] used gas-chromatography to analyze the effluent after TCF treatment.

## Instruments and Software



- [[2016-sidaoui|Sidaoui, 2016]] used gas-chromatography to analyze the chemical composition of waste oil.

- [[2020-mohammed|Mohammed, 2020]] used gas_chromatography to confirm the generation of nitrogen gas during thermochemical reactions.

- [[2020-usman|Usman, 2020]] applied gas chromatography to determine CPI and Pr/Ph ratios in bitumen extracts.

- [[2023-al-eid|Al-Eid, 2023]] applied gas-chromatography to analyze hydrocarbon composition and detect evaporative loss.

- [[2024-moh-ali|Moh-Ali, 2024]] used gas-chromatography to analyze the effluent after TCF treatment.

## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- The system was calibrated using standards of thiophene, BT, and DBT (1 to 75 ppm). ([[2015-danmaliki|Danmaliki, 2015, p. 86]])

- Used to select the appropriate emulsifier agent for the specific waste oil chemistry. ([[2016-sidaoui|Sidaoui, 2016, p. 111]])

- Gas chromatography confirmed the production of nitrogen gas as a result of the chemical reaction between ammonium chloride and sodium nitrite. ([[2020-mohammed|Mohammed, 2020, p. 119]])

- CPI values ranged from 0.9 to 1.4 across various lithofacies samples. ([[2020-usman|Usman, 2020, p. 58]])

- Differentiation of the FID pyrogram into parts allowed identification of volatile, heavy oil, and kerogen products. ([[2023-al-eid|Al-Eid, 2023, p. 104]])

- Analysis revealed the presence of light hydrocarbons, confirming the conversion of kerogen into hydrocarbons. ([[2024-moh-ali|Moh-Ali, 2024, p. 99]])
